data.title: Bitcoin’s chance of hitting $125K by June rises as traders bid on upside: Derive
data.description:
Bitcoin has lingered below the psychological $100,000 level for seven days, but a crypto researcher says there’s a nearly 50% chance it will surge to $125,000 by late June.

The chance of Bitcoin hitting $125,000 by the middle of this year “has improved to 44.4%, up from 41.9%,” onchain options protocol Derive head of research Dr. Sean Dawson said in a Feb. 13 markets report.

Bitcoin odds for a major downside are much lower

Dawson added that the chance of BTC touching $75,000 before June had dropped to 12.1%, down from 17.8%. Meanwhile, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes is more bearish on the downside.

Hayes predicted last month that Bitcoin could potentially pull back toward the $70,000 to $75,000 range, a move he said may trigger a “mini financial crisis.”

Bitcoin last traded around $75,000 on Nov. 8, just three days after US President Donald Trump won the election — a moment widely seen as the catalyst for a month-long rally that pushed Bitcoin to $100,000 for the first time on Dec. 5.

At the time of publication, Bitcoin is trading at $97,128, according to CoinMarketCap.

Bitcoin has been trading below $100,000 since Feb. 7. Bitcoin briefly tapped a new all-time high of $109,000 on Jan. 20, prior to Trump’s inauguration.

Market sentiment “Neutral,” chop may continue

Crypto trader Jelle said that until Bitcoin reclaims $100,000, “choppy conditions” will remain.

The crypto market sentiment measuring Crypto Fear & Greed Index shows sentiment on Feb. 14 was “Neutral” with a score of 48 out of a total possible of 100.

Asset manager VanEck said in December that the bull market will hit a “medium-term peak” in the first quarter of 2025 before surging to all-time highs by the end of the year.

It projected that “at the cycle’s apex,” Bitcoin would trade at around $180,000 while ETH would trade above $6,000.”

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: Julphar Swings to Preliminary Net Profit in FY24; Revenue Rise
data.description:
Gulf Pharmaceutical Industries (ADX:JULPHAR), d/b/a Julphar, on Friday reported a preliminary net profit in 2024, while revenue from continuing operations jumped year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 40.5 million Emirati dirhams, compared with a net loss of 98.5 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.035 dirham from a loss per share of 0.086 dirham.

The pharmaceutical company's revenue from continuing operations was 1.31 billion dirhams, compared with 1.28 billion dirhams previously.

data.title: GKB Logs Lower FY24 Profit; Operating Income Up
data.description:
Graubündner Kantonalbank , d/b/a GKB, said Friday profit for 2024 slipped while operating income rose year over year.

Group profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 229.5 million francs, compared with 230.6 million francs earlier. EPS moved to 96.4 francs from 97.6 francs.

The Swiss cantonal bank's operating income was 528.4 million francs, compared with 525.9 million francs a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared an unchanged dividend of 47.50 francs per participation certificate for the period.

For 2025, GKB expects consolidated profit in the range of 210 million francs and 230 million francs.

data.title: Thomson Medical Group Unlikely to Turn Profitable Soon — Market Talk
data.description: Thomson Medical Group is unlikely to turn profitable in the short term, Maybank Research's Eric Ong says in a report. The Singapore-listed company posted a fiscal 1H net loss of S$12.9 million, with the magnitude of the loss catching the analyst off guard. The poor performance of its Malaysian operations and increased interest expenses related to the acquisition of FV Hospital in Vietnam were the main contributors. The healthcare-services provider probably needs more time to fully reap the benefits of maximizing its assets and enhancing margins in its key markets, Ong adds. The brokerage downgrades the stock to hold from buy and cuts the target price to S$0.05 from S$0.06. Shares are 2.2% lower at S$0.044. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: I Synergy Group Announces Debt Assignment As it Pursues Board Review
data.description:
I Synergy Group assigned an unsecured AU$1 million loan agreement to its non-executive director Dato' Hoo Voon Him on the same terms as that were announced with the loan on March 28, 2023, as part of its board and corporate review, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

As of Friday, AU$395,296.68 had been drawn down from the facility or AU$405,135.68, including interest.

The company originally entered into the loan agreement with its executive chairman Dato' Teo Chee Hong.

Furthermore, Dato' Teo Chee Hong disposed of 50 million fully-paid ordinary shares at AU$0.012 per share via off-market transfer to its managing director Heng Jee Meng, to continue to fund the investment requirements set by the Australian Government, upon the business talent visa granted to Dato' Teo Chee Hong.

data.title: Chicago soybeans rise on Argentina weather woes, corn flat
data.description:
Key points:
  • Wheat edges up amid strong exports, weather risks
  • Traders watch developments surrounding U.S. tariffs
  • U.S. efforts in Russia-Ukraine peace talks in focus
  • Corn set for 2nd week of gains, wheat up for 6th week

Chicago soybean futures rose for a second session on Friday, underpinned by weather concerns in key supplier Argentina, although weak exports and progress in Brazilian harvest kept the market on track for a weekly decline.

Corn prices were poised for a second week of gains due to tighter global stocks, strong demand, and Argentina weather woes. Wheat was set for a sixth week of gains, supported by strong exports and freeze risks in key growing regions.

Soybeans gained 0.19% to $10.32 per bushel by 0442 GMT, but were down 1.67% for the week. Corn remained unchanged at $4.93 per bushel, but was up 1.18% for the week despite expectation of higher production in Brazil.

On Thursday, Brazil's crop agency Conab lowered its soybean supply forecast in the world's top supplier due to dry weather in the south, but raised corn estimates.

Argentina's dry conditions boosted soybean and corn prices, though rains have eased the dryness.

Weak Chinese demand and expectations of a bumper harvest in Brazil have weighed on soybean prices.

Weekly U.S. soybean exports were below expectations, while corn sales exceeded forecasts, according to a report from the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) on Thursday.

The grain market remained cautious as traders awaited U.S. President Donald Trump's efforts to negotiate an end to the Ukraine war and updates on tariff changes with key trading partners.

Andrew Whitelaw, an analyst at Episode 3 in Canberra, said that while the Russian-Ukraine peace talks will impact the market, their effect on supply chains and agricultural production is still some time away.

If the sanctions against Russia end, it may be bearish for the European market as Russian products may re-enter the market, said Johnny Xiang, founder of Chinese consulting firm AgRader.

On Thursday, the U.S. moved to raise decades-old low tariff rates to match those of other countries, citing Brazil's ethanol tariffs as an example of unfair trade practices. Most of U.S. ethanol comes from corn.

"It absolutely will swing back demand to domestically produced ethanol rather than Brazilian imports due to the differential in tariffs if these tariffs become equal. But it's a wait and see," Whitelaw said.

Wheat climbed 1.13% to $5.84 a bushel, extending weekly gains with a 0.26% rise, partly supported by Saudi Arabia's tender to buy an estimated 595,000 metric tons, analysts said.

Traders are monitoring cold fronts in the U.S. Plains and Black Sea for freeze risks, though snow forecasts may protect the crop.

data.title: Market Chatter: US Tariffs on Chinese Steel Affects Market, Chinese Steel Body Says
data.description:
China's Iron and Steel Association criticized US President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum, saying it is leaving an"adverse impact" on the international steel industry, Reuters reported, citing the organization.

The association said the move will reduce the competitiveness of China's steel exports as other countries may follow the US' new tariffs, affecting competition within the global steel industry, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Deepak Nitrite Sees Sharp Decline in Fiscal Q3 Consolidated Net Profit; Shares Sink 15%
data.description:
Deepak Nitrite's consolidated attributable net profit plunged to 980.9 million Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 2.02 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share contracted to 7.19 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 14.81 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the chemical intermediates manufacturer.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 fell to 19.0 billion rupees from 20.09 billion rupees a year earlier.

Shares of the company were down 15% in recent trade.

data.title: New York Times business news - February 14
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the New York Times business pages. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- TikTok returned on the U.S. app stores of Apple and Google on Thursday, as President Donald Trump delayed its ban until April 5 and assured the companies they would not be fined for distributing or maintaining the Chinese app.

- Banks including Morgan Stanley on Thursday sold about $4.7 billion of X’s debt according to two people familiar with the transaction.

- Co-founder of Airbnb Joe Gebbia is taking a role in Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

- Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy on Thursday said that he had asked Kelly Ortberg, CEO of Boeing to come to Washington and update him on the company’s commitment to addressing its quality and safety issues.

data.title: BII Railway Transportation Technology to Divest 49% Stake in Transport Controlling Systems JV
data.description:
BII Railway Transportation Technology will sell its 49% stake in Metro Technology through public tender on the China Beijing Equity Exchange, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

Metro Technology, a public transport networking and controlling systems company, is a joint venture that is 49% held by BII Railway Transportation Technology and 51% held by Beijing Subway.

The minimum consideration is slated to be 68.3 million yuan, the filing said.

BII Railway will use the proceeds from the divestment to repay its loans, and fund working capital and future investments.

Shares rose 2% during Friday's afternoon trading.

data.title: India Stocks Set to Close Week on Low Note
data.description:
India's equities fell by 311 points, or 0.4%, to 75,832, touching their nearly three-week low in early deals on Friday, after being flat in the previous session.

Almost all sectors traded in the red, led by pharmaceuticals.

Global trade uncertainty, ongoing foreign fund outflows, and disappointing Q3 earnings results continued to pressure sentiment, partly offset by easing domestic inflation.

Traders are awaiting wholesale price data for January, with the market expecting it to accelerate to a seven-month high of 2.5%.

However, a rally on Wall Street Thursday and hopes of easing the trade war following the Modi-Trump talks capped the falls.

The Nifty 50 dropped 0.5% below 23,000, with the bottom performers being Adani Ports (-2.4%), UltraTech Cement (-2.0%), and Sun Pharmaceuticals (-1.9%).

For the week, the index is heading for a 2.5% slump, the first weekly decline in three weeks, amid concerns over a potential global trade war.

data.title: China’s 10-Year Yield Hits 3-Week High
data.description:
China's 10-year government bond yield rose to around 1.65%, hitting its highest level in three weeks, as investors digested the People's Bank of China's latest policy signals.

The central bank pledged to adjust monetary policy when appropriate to support economic growth, emphasizing liquidity management and targeted measures to stabilize the financial system.

Despite this, bond yields climbed as market participants assessed the potential impact of these policy moves.

Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump delayed the implementation of reciprocal tariffs, easing concerns about escalating trade tensions, while the US continues to investigate unfair trade practices, with China among its key targets.

In response, Chinese producers are diversifying away from the U.S. market, focusing on Europe and emerging markets, which could lead to price wars abroad and economic challenges at home.

data.title: Al Ain Ahlia Insurance Swings to Preliminary Net Loss in FY24; Revenue Declines
data.description:
Al Ain Ahlia Insurance (ADX:ALAIN) reported on Friday a preliminary net loss for full year 2024 while insurance revenue decreased year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 31.4 million Emirati dirhams, compared with a net profit of 34.6 million dirhams earlier. Loss per share came in at 2.09 dirhams from an EPS of 2.31 dirhams.

The Abu Dhabi-listed insurance company's insurance revenue was 1.14 billion dirhams, compared with 1.43 billion dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Market Chatter: Sun Hung Kai to Launch 781 Units in Sai Sha, Hong Kong in Q1
data.description:
Sun Hung Kai Properties is set to launch 781 units at its Sierra Sea project in Sai Sha, Hong Kong in the first quarter, The Standard reported Friday.

The project has an area of about 8.1 million square feet with 10,000 units on offer, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Koda Returns to Profit in Fiscal H1; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Koda returned to a profit attributable to owners of the company in the fiscal half ended Dec. 31, 2024, to $416,000 from an attributable loss of $2.3 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Earnings per share came in at $0.0050 compared with a loss per share of $0.0282 in the comparable period.

Revenue surged 39% year over year to $27.7 million from about $20.0 million, mainly due to higher export sales to new customers in US.

Shares of the furniture producer and supplier were up over 4% in recent trading.

data.title: SEC’s Lawsuit against Binance Is Officially on Hold
data.description:
A federal judge yesterday (Thursday) ordered a hold on the Securities and Exchange Commission’s (SEC) lawsuit against crypto exchange giant Binance for 60 days. The order followed a joint request by both parties.

Relying on the Crypto Task Force

In their joint motion earlier this week, the two parties highlighted that the newly formed task force to review crypto regulations might "impact and facilitate the potential resolution of this case."

The regulator formed the crypto task force last month, led by Republican Commissioner Hester Peirce, a staunch supporter of cryptocurrencies. It is also one of the first moves made by the agency under the interim leadership of Mark Uyeda, who replaced Gary Gensler as Acting Chair.

President Donald Trump has already nominated Paul Atkins, a Washington lawyer and former Commissioner at the agency, as Gensler’s permanent replacement. However, his appointment is pending Senate approval. Unlike Gensler, who opened many high-profile cases against crypto companies, Atkins is seen as crypto-friendly.

SEC’s Move against the Crypto Giant

The SEC sued Binance and its founder, Changpeng Zhao, in mid-2023, alleging that the exchange artificially inflated trading volumes, misused customer funds, and misled investors about its monitoring controls.

"The SEC’s case has always been without merit, and we are eager to put this behind us and continue our focus on keeping Binance the most secure, licensed, and trusted exchange in the world," a Binance spokesperson said earlier.

In separate cases brought by the Department of Justice and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), the crypto exchange paid a total of over $7.1 billion in settlements and agreed to exit the US. Zhao also had to step down from his role as Binance's CEO and spent four months in prison.

Meanwhile, under the new leadership, the SEC is reportedly scaling down its crypto enforcement division, which has more than 50 lawyers.

data.title: Santander Upgrade Prompts Fitch to Raise Santander UK's Shareholder Support Rating
data.description:
Fitch increased on Thursday the shareholder support rating of Santander UK Group to a- from bbb+, citing the upgrade of parent Banco Santander .

The rating agency said the higher rating reflects the Spanish lender's improved capacity to support its UK operations, as evidenced by the recent upgrade in its long-term issuer default rating to A from A-.

data.title: FX option expiries for 14 February 10am New York cut
data.description:
There are quite a number of large ones to watch out for, as highlighted in bold.

The first ones are for EUR/USD with some large bookends at 1.0400 and 1.0500 in particular. In between that, there are also some larger ones layered between 1.0435 through to 1.0475 on the day.

The dollar is in a weak spot this week as traders have largely shrugged off tariff fears, as evident by the reaction even to Trump's reciprocal tariffs yesterday. That is putting the near-term bias in favour of buyers. As such, the expiries above should offer some pull in keeping price action more underpinned in the session ahead.

The ones at 1.0500 is likely to keep a lid on things, holding at the January highs as well.

Then, there is one for USD/JPY at the 152.00 level. The pair is contesting with its 200-day moving average of 152.68, so that's the bigger draw at the moment. The expiries might not see much impact unless the bond market also plays ball, which usually only comes closer to US trading as seen yesterday.

There's also one for USD/CAD at the 1.4200 level but I wouldn't attach too much significance to it. It could help to draw in price action if risk sentiment is more tentative in European trading. However, the figure level doesn't offer any technical significance at this stage. The break below the support region of 1.4260-80 has freed up plenty of room to the downside for the pair with 1.4100 eyed next.

And lastly, there is one for AUD/USD at the 0.6300 level. The pair itself is nudging close to the January high of 0.6330 and that's the more crucial technical point to watch now. As such, the expiries might not feature much into play besides the potential to hold price action thereabouts in the meantime.

But if risk starts running again and the bond market also starts moving, those will be bigger drivers of trading sentiment as we look to close out the week.

For more information on how to use this data, you may refer to this post here.

data.title: Ecofibre Reaches Agreement For Loan Term Extensions with Australian Unsecured Lenders
data.description:
Ecofibre reached an agreement with its two unsecured lenders in Australia to extend the term of their loans, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Its existing AU$7 million loan granted by James & Cordelia Thiele Trust Fund was partially due on Jan. 1, with the balance payable on July 15, the filing said.

It repaid AU$500,000 out of AU$1 million due in January, and the term of the remaining debt has now been extended.

The facility amount is now AU$6.5 million and the interest rate is 14%, which will be increased if the Reserve Bank of Australia's cash rate exceeds 5%.

Payments of AU$650,000 are now due on Jan. 1, 2026, and AU$750,000 are due on Jan. 1, 2027, with the balance due on Jan. 1, 2028.

The existing loan from Lambert Superannuation Fund was fully repayable on July 15 and has been extended as well.

The facility amount is now AU$3.5 million and the interest rate is 10%.

A AU$10,000 payment is due every month from April to June and AU$30,000 per month thereafter until the loan is repaid.

data.title: Global Lithium Resources Downgraded to Neutral from Overweight by Barrenjoey
Singapore's Economy Expands 5% in Q4 2024
data.description:
Singapore's economy expanded by 5% year over year during the fourth quarter of 2024, moderating from a 5.7% growth in the preceding quarter, according to data released by the Ministry of Trade and Industry on Friday.

On a quarter over quarter seasonally adjusted basis, the city-state's economy expanded by 0.5%, compared to the 3.0% expansion during the third quarter of 2024.

GDP growth for 2024 stood at 4.4%, an improvement on the 1.8% expansion in 2023, thanks to growth in the wholesale trade, finance and insurance and manufacturing sectors.

Meanwhile, Singapore has maintained the GDP growth forecast for 2025 at 1.0% to 3.0% for 2025, according to the report.

data.title: Barclays Faces New Money Laundering Probe; Sets Aside GBP90 Million Provision for FCA Review
data.description:
Barclays said with its 2024 results on Thursday that it is facing a new money-laundering probe from the UK's Financial Conduct Authority while also disclosing a provision of 90 million pounds sterling for a separate FCA probe.

The new civil enforcement investigation from the FCA is mainly focused on the historical oversight and management of certain customers with increased risk. The British bank said it is cooperating with the regulator for the investigation.

The provision of 90 million pounds is related to the FCA's ongoing review of historical motor finance commission arrangements. Barclays noted that it stopped operating in the motor finance market in 2019 but its previous operations may be covered by potential a redress scheme by the FCA.

The provision is based on currently available information and is subject to uncertainty as the FCA's review remains ongoing.

data.title: Watania International Posts Higher Preliminary FY24 Net Profit; Revenue Fall
data.description:
Watania International (DFM:WATANIA) said Friday that its preliminary net profit for full year 2024 rose, while takaful revenue declined year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 14.6 million Emirati dirhams compared with 13.3 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.058 dirham from 0.051 dirham.

The investment holding company's takaful revenue was 869.6 million dirhams, compared with 954.4 million dirhams earlier.

data.title: Tariff worries wane, Hermes earnings await
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

Investors are breathing a sigh of relief for now as the roadmap for Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs suggested room for negotiations, while results from luxury firm Hermes will be the main event rounding out a series of strong earnings in Europe.

Asian markets also took their cues from Wall Street's positive reaction to U.S. inflation data, which suggested that prices remain consistent with the Federal Reserve's target range, while Chinese tech shares extended their rally after home-grown start-up DeepSeek's breakthrough last month.

Hong Kong's benchmark index was perched near a four-month high and on course for its best week since September. It's also on a five-week winning streak, the longest since 2022.

The Hang Seng's 11% rise so far this year has made it easily the best performing major stock market in Asia and is among the top globally. Germany's DAX index is leading the way with a 14% gain this year, hitting a record high on Thursday.

Swiss , French and Spanish benchmarks are not far behind, having clocked double-digit gains so far in 2025.

In corporate news, earnings from Hermes will take the spotlight in Europe, where investors are keen to learn whether the Birkin bag maker remains best-placed to weather a downturn in the luxury sector, given its wealthier customer base.

Luxury goods companies have been grappling with their slowest sales in years, as consumers in China tighten their belts, although upbeat results from Burberry , Cartier owner Richemont and others have fuelled hopes the sector is starting the year on firmer ground.

Traders in Europe may lock in profits after a strong run this week, with the pan-European STOXX 600 index closing at a record high every day this week and up almost 10% year-to-date. Futures point to a lower open for European stock markets.

For investors, the focus will be on the tariff saga after a directive from Trump stopped short of imposing fresh duties, and instead kicked off what could be weeks or months of investigation into levies imposed on U.S. goods.

And finally, a valentine's day gift for TikTok fans in the United States as the popular short-video app returned to the app stores of Apple and Google.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

- Euro zone Q4 GDP and employment data

- Hermes annual results

data.title: Gain Plus Chairman and CEO Resigns; Successor Named
data.description:
Gain Plus Holdings Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Tsang Chiu Kwan resigned effective Thursday, according to a same-day filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

Tsang will stay on as director during the transition.

The construction services company named Wong Howard to succeed Tsang as chairman and CEO, the filing said.

Shares rose 2% during Friday's afternoon trading.

data.title: China's Central Bank to Adjust Monetary Policies to Keep Ample Liquidity
data.description:
The People's Bank of China plans to use various monetary policy tools and adjust the intensity and timing of policies to keep ample liquidity, Xinhua reported Thursday, citing a report from the central bank.

The PBOC has trimmed the reserve requirement ratio 29 times since 2013, decreasing the average RRR to 6.6% from 20.1%. It has also conducted government bond purchases amounting to 1 trillion yuan in 2024 to maintain ample liquidity, the report said.

data.title: Gulf Cement's Preliminary Net Loss Declines in FY24; Revenue Grows
data.description:
Gulf Cement (ADX:GCEM) said Thursday its preliminary net loss for the full year 2024 decreased, while revenue increased year over year.

Net loss for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 41.5 million dirhams, compared with 51.6 million dirhams earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.10 dirham from 0.13 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed cement manufacturer's revenue was 480.9 million dirhams, compared with 462.7 million dirhams a year ago.
